threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You can spot a newbie when they still think it's a good idea to take the tube during a strike. Just wait till the buses show up.
Heard a guy on the 59 bus trying to impress a girl with tales of his 'wild' nights at the Ritzy. Turns out, his idea of wild is a Tuesday quiz night.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When the BVG strike left my street eerily quiet, but I still heard the distant sounds of a spontaneous block party. That's Berlin.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You ever notice how the U-Bahn station at Kottbusser Tor always has a different street artist's work plastered under the escalator? It's like a mini gallery on your commute.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only locals know that the BART maintenance delays are just another excuse not to ride the 14 or 49 during rush hour. We just laugh it off.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a pigeon hop on the 31-Balboa just as the doors closed. It looked around, then strutted down the aisle like it owned the place. Classic Muni.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about parks and plazas, but it’s the late-night chiringuitos and quirky shops that give it life after dark.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell a newcomer by the way they struggle to pronounce 'Gràcia' — it’s 'gra-thia', not 'gra-sia', folks.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'the Tube'; locals just say 'the underground' or 'the train' like they own it.
Saw a bloke on the 208 talking to a kebab while waiting for his stop, arguing about the toppings. Classic Bromley vibes.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I saw a cat lounging on the tram tracks at Karl-Marx-Strasse while everyone just walked by—only in Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg will you find a late-night Döner spot that doubles as an art gallery. I swear, I once saw a performance art piece while waiting for my kebab.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know they’re from here when they dodge the street performer with a half-eaten burrito, but still stop for a quick chat with the taco truck guy.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a tuxedo on the 19 Polk, scrolling through wedding pics while the bus lurched. Only in San Francisco does your big day start with Muni chaos.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all the best tapas are by La Rambla. Meanwhile, locals flock to the tiny bars on Carrer de Fraternitat for the real flavors.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones still carrying a map, oblivious to the shortcut through Plaça de la Virreina that everyone else knows.
